mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements that boost equity valuations and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations within the specified recovery regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase household income and consumer spending expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show a Rank IC below 0.01 for 5-day forward returns across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy compresses real returns and forces equity deleveraging, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate profit forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly declines produce positive 20-day returns in over 50% of cases within the late-cycle regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly contraction in industrial production below the 3-month moving average indicates supply-side shocks that immediately depress equity prices over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average.
